rulings.Case Title: VALERIE BEHRENDT VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Judge Goldsmith is recused. This matter will be heard before Judge Busch on the same date and time (February 14, 2014 at 9:30 am) in Dept. 504. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. The tentative ruling is as follows: The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges an aiding and abetting theory of liability against defendant Wells Fargo. Plaintiff alleges that defendant knew of Mr. Pitto's wrongdoing and gave substantial assistance to him. See Casey v. U.S. Bank National Ass'n (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 1138, 1144. The Court denies the parties' requests for judicial notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHINGLE SPRINGS TRIBAL GAMING AUTHORITY et al VS. HOEM & ASSOCIATES, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT RUDOLPH AND SLETTEN, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted with ten days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for equitable indemnity. Granted without leave to amend as to the second and sixth causes of action for contribution and declaratory relief. In order to be liable for equitable indemnity, there must be potential for joint and several liability in the underlying action. (BFGC Architects Planners, Inc. v. Forcum/Mackey Construction, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 848, 853, "The doctrine applies only among defendants who are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiff.") In other words, had the plaintiff in the underlying action sued the party against whom equitable indemnity is sought against, the plaintiff would have the potential to recover against that party in tort. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held jointly and severally liable for intentional or negligent misrepresentation, the torts alleged in the underlying complaint. No facts are alleged that Cross-Defendant made any representation to the plaintiff in the underlying case. Additionally, assuming breach of warranty is a tort cause of action, the FACC does not plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held liable for the breach of implied warranty of merchantability or fitness. The FACC does not plead that Cross-Defendants sold any goods to the underlying plaintiff. (See Fogo v. Cutter Laboratories, Inc. (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 744, 758, "¿a sale is ordinarily an essential element of any warranty, express or implied.") Cross-Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts, if it can in good faith, establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held jointly and severally liable in tort in the underlying action. As to the second cause of action, the FACC does not plead that a money judgment has been jointly rendered against Cross-Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant. (See CCP sec. 875.) As to the sixth cause of action, the only controversy alleged between the parties is whether Cross-Plaintiff is entitled to equitable indemnity, and therefore, this cause of action is duplicative. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three And Document Demands, Set Three And Req. For Sanctions Against Armada In The Amount Of $4900.00; Appendix And Statement Of Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three And Document Demands, Set Three And Request For Sanctions Against Armada In The Amount Of $4900.00. (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. (Continued to Part 2). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Travelers Casualty Insurance Company Of America'S Motion To Compel Defendant'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three And Document Demands, Set Three And Request For Sanctions Against Armada In The Amount Of $4900.00 (Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three And Document Demands, Set Three And Request For Sanctions Against Armada In The Amount Of $4900.00. (Part 2 of 2). The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Armada Protective Services' responses to Travelers' Request for Production Nos. 35, 36, 37, 38, and 39 consist solely of objections, contrary to the requirements of CCP Section 2031.240(a). All five requests seek information that is relevant to the subject matter of the pending action. If only part of an item or category of item in a demand for inspection is objectionable, the response shall contain a statement of compliance, or a representation of inability to comply with respect to the remainder of that item or category. Armada Protective Services cannot simply refuse to produce any documents in response to demands that it maintains are unduly burdensome. CCP Section 2031.240(a) imposes a duty to conduct a diligent search and reasonable inquiry in response to the demands and then provide a statement of compliance describing what the responding party is willing to produce, or a statement of inability to comply meeting the requirements of CCP Section 2031.230. Armada's privacy arguments are not well taken given the allegation in the First Amended complaint at Paragraph 8 that Defendant's General Manager Vladimir Butenko provided Plaintiff Traveler's Insurance with information concerning ArmPro Securit's payroll in connection with Plaintiff's audit of Defendant Armada Protective Services, and Defendant's counsel's admission that Mr. Butenko has access to ArmPro Security's payroll record. Defendant Armada's responses to the interrogatories, consisting solely of objections, are also not well taken. A party may not construe an interrogatory to create an ambiguity. "Indeed, even where a question is somewhat ambiguous, but the nature of the information sought is apparent, the proper solution is to provide an appropriate response." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal. App. 3d 771, 783. Traveler's request for sanctions is DENIED. The declarations supporting the request have lumped together compensable and non-compensable time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bmw Of North America, Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 10, Defendant Bmw Of North America, Llc's Motion For Summary Judgment. The Court grants defendant BMW's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff fails to establish a design defect in the motorcycle or causation. Defendant maintained its initial burden of production by citing plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses and also by relying on the declarations of Mr. Yeldham and Dr. Kessler. In opposition, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact regarding design defect or causation. The Court sustains defendant's objection to the declaration of Dr. Rutchik as lacking foundation. The Court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance under CCP 437c(h). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate diligence, he does not highlight what facts he hopes to find to create a triable issue of material fact, the court previously granted plaintiff a continuance, and another continuance is not feasible in light of the current trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Corbin-Pacific, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 10, Defendant Corbin-Pacific, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment. The Court grants defendant Corbin-Pacific's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff fails to establish a design defect in the motorcycle seat or causation. Defendant maintained its initial burden of production by relying on the declarations of Mr. Yeldham, Mr. Corbin, and Dr. Kessler. In opposition, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact regarding design defect or causation. The Court sustains defendant's objection to the declaration of Dr. Rutchik as lacking foundation. The Court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance under CCP 437c(h). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate diligence, he does not highlight what facts he hopes to find to create a triable issue of material fact, the court previously granted plaintiff a continuance, and another continuance is not feasible in light of the current trial date. rulings.Case Title: URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS, A VS. UNIVERSAL MOLDING EXTRUSION COMPANY, INC. DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT TERVITA CORPORATION, A CANADIAN CORPORATION's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion to quash is granted and cross-defendant Tervita Corporation is dismissed. First, plaintiff fails to establish minimum contacts to support a finding of general or specific jurisdiction. Tervita Corporation is a Canadian entity that was retained to perform work in Canada. Second, plaintiff fails to show that it properly served Tervita Corporation's agent for service of process. Third, the Court denies UMEX et al.'s request for jurisdictional discovery. UMEX et al. do not suggest that additional discovery will reveal additional California contacts and allowing the discovery will lead to another trial continuance. rulings.Case Title: WHITNEY R LEEMAN PH.D. VS. MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED's Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For A Protective Order. (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. Plaintiff noticed the entity deposition to take place within the county where the action is pending, a proper location under CCP Section 2025.250(d). The Second District Court of Appeal's decision in Toyota Motor Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2011) 197 Cal. App. 4th 1107, 1110 does not control. In that case, the Court of Appeal held that CCP Section 1989 prevented a court from compelling a natural person who resided outside of California to travel from Japan to California for a deposition. The court held that while Section 2025.260(a) permits depositions more than 75 (or 150) miles from a deponent's residence, Section 1989 restricts a deponent from being required to attend a California deposition if the deponent is not a California resident. Toyota Motor Corp. v. Sup. Ct., 197 Cal. App. 4th at 1113. But see Glass v. Sup. Ct. (Cal. App. 4th Dist. 1988) 204 Cal. App. 3d 1048, 1051 (plaintiff Indiana Corporation's out-of-state officers and directors could be compelled to sit for depositions in California). (Continued to Part 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WHITNEY R LEEMAN PH.D. VS. MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mccormick & Company, Incorporated'S Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For A Protective Order (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For A Protective Order (Part 2 of 2). The Toyota decision was made in the context of individuals whose depositions had been noticed, not a corporation commanded to produce an agent to testify pursuant to CCP Section 2025.230. The Toyota Court explicitly cautioned, "we express no opinion, however, as to whether our analysis or the conclusions we have reached in this opinion would or should extend or apply to a court order made pursuant to section 2025.230 which provides for the circumstance where the deponent named is not a natural person." Toyota Motor Corp. v. Sup. Ct., 197 Cal. App. 4th at 1125, fn. 20. Section 1989 appears on its face to apply to natural persons given that entities themselves are not witnesses, but rather speak through the agents they designate pursuant to CCP Section 2023.230. Thus Section 1989 does not appear to be a limitation on the ability of a court to compel an entity to produce its agents for deposition at a location in California specified in CCP Section 2025.250. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PERSOLVE LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DBA VS. CECELIA HENRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 14, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT CECELIA HENRY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Granted pursuant to the stipulation entered into between Plaintiff and Defendant. Defendant shall file her proposed answer within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
